title: Tactus MessageHistoryManager auto-compacts when history exceeds budget

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
**As a** Tactus agent developer  
**I want** MessageHistoryManager to automatically compact history when it exceeds token budget  
**So that** any agent can maintain long conversations without manual overflow handling

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: Automatic history compaction in Tactus
  MessageHistoryManager transparently compacts message history
  when it exceeds configured token budget.

  Scenario: No compaction when under budget
    Given a MessageHistoryManager with max_tokens=8000
    And auto_compact=True
    And a message history with 6000 estimated tokens
    When get_history() is called
    Then the full uncompacted history is returned
    And no compaction is triggered

  Scenario: Rolling summary compaction when over budget
    Given a MessageHistoryManager with RollingSummaryStrategy
    And max_tokens=8000, recent_window=10
    And a message history with 15000 estimated tokens
    When get_history() is called
    Then compaction is triggered automatically
    And system messages are preserved at the start
    And the last 10 messages are preserved uncompacted
    And middle messages are summarized into a SYSTEM message
    And the returned history fits within 8000 tokens
    And compaction_metadata is stored (summary, tokens_saved)

  Scenario: Pluggable compaction strategies
    Given a MessageHistoryManager with a custom CompactionStrategy
    And the strategy has should_compact() and compact() methods
    When get_history() is called on overbudget history
    Then the custom strategy's compact() method is invoked
    And the returned history respects the strategy's behavior

  Scenario: Compaction metadata is accessible
    Given a MessageHistoryManager that has performed compaction
    When I query compaction metadata for an agent
    Then I receive summary text, tokens_before, tokens_after
    And messages_summarized and messages_retained counts
```
